nodes.title: REPLACEMENT MODE — mute = OFF (animate full body) · active = ON (face into real video)
MODEL LOADERS (WanAnimate · VAE · CLIP-Vision · umt5 · LoRAs · Uni3C)
PREPROCESS (pose · ref resize · clip-vision · uni3c · text-embeds)
📖 How REPLACEMENT MODE works
widgets_values:
# 🔁 REPLACEMENT MODE

Toggle the **yellow subgraph node** below (right‑click → Mode) to switch how your reference character is used.

## ⬛ OFF  (node = **Mute**)
Animates the **entire reference image** — the character, outfit *and* its background — to match the pose/face/motion of the driving video. The driving clip is used only as a **motion source**. Use this for "make my character perform this dance" on the image's own background.

## 🟨 ON  (node = **Active**)
SAM3 segments the **person** in the driving video, and the model paints **your character into that masked region** while keeping the **real video's background, lighting and framing**. Use this to "drop my character into the actual clip" (person swap).

---
**How it works:** the subgraph outputs `bg_images` + `mask` into *Animate Embeds*. Muted → those inputs are empty → full‑image animation. Active → they drive in‑painting over the real footage. The **Mask Preview** node shows what's being replaced.

**Tips**
- Keep **REACTOR FACE LOCK** on for a consistent first‑frame identity in both modes.
- ON mode follows the real clip's body/crop — a full‑body driving video gives the best result.
- Only one mode renders at a time; flip the node and re‑queue to compare.
- Toggle this node’s Mode (right-click -> Mode): Active = ON, Mute or Bypass = OFF. Ships OFF (full-body animate).

groups.title: REPLACEMENT MODE — toggle the subgraph node (mute = OFF / active = ON)
